description: Calculate the money-weighted rate of return (internal rate of return) on a portfolio with external cash flows, reflecting both performance and investor timing

fields.name: cash_flows
fields.label: Array of signed cash flows, beginning with initial investment (negative outflow) at t=0

fields.name: cash_flow_dates
fields.label: Optional dates aligned to cash_flows for irregular (XIRR) calculation

fields.name: initial_guess
fields.label: Initial IRR guess for numerical solver (default 0.10)

core_definition.mwr: Rate r such that NPV(cash_flows, r) = 0

solver_characteristics.nonlinear: Equation is polynomial in (1+r) of degree T; no closed form
solver_characteristics.newton_raphson: Preferred method; converges quickly from reasonable initial guess
solver_characteristics.bisection_fallback: Use when Newton-Raphson oscillates or diverges
solver_characteristics.multiple_roots: Descartes' rule — up to (sign changes in CF) real roots; choose economically meaningful (typically unique > -1)

sign_convention.terminal_value: Positive (final portfolio value as liquidating inflow)

appropriate_use.investor_perspective: MWR measures return earned by the specific investor given their timing of cash flows
appropriate_use.not_comparable_across_managers: Two managers with identical underlying performance can show different MWRs if their clients' cash flows differ — use TWR for manager comparison

validation.min_two_flows: At least two cash flows required (initial investment + terminal value)
validation.sign_change: At least one sign change required for a meaningful IRR

compute_mwr.description: Solve for IRR when cash flow series valid

no_sign_change.description: All cash flows same sign (no IRR exists)

insufficient_flows.description: Fewer than two cash flows provided

errors.message: Unable to converge to an IRR within tolerance; inspect cash flow sign pattern
IRR undefined — cash flows must include at least one sign change
At least two cash flows (investment and terminal value) required

anti_patterns.rule: Do not report MWR when evaluating an investment manager's skill
anti_patterns.why: MWR conflates manager skill with investor's cash flow timing; use time-weighted-return for manager evaluation

anti_patterns.rule: Do not assume a unique IRR when there are multiple sign changes in the cash flow stream
anti_patterns.why: Descartes' rule permits multiple real roots; report all economically meaningful roots or switch to MIRR

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use MWR to rank mutually exclusive projects of different scale
anti_patterns.why: IRR ranking can contradict NPV ranking for projects with different scale or cash flow timing

anti_patterns.rule: Do not annualize MWR by multiplying a sub-period rate

goals.description: Calculate the money-weighted rate of return (internal rate of return) on a portfolio with external cash flows, reflecting both performance and investor timing

success_metrics.measurement: All decisions have complete audit trails

constraints.description: All operations must be auditable and traceable

tradeoffs.reason: trading operations require precise execution and full audit trails

invariants: error messages never expose internal system details

worked_example.scenario: Year 0: buy 1 share @ 0; Year 1: receive  dividend, buy 1 more share @ 0; Year 2: receive  dividend, sell 2 shares @ 5 each
worked_example.interpretation: IRR ≈ 9.68% per year accounting for investor's purchase timing

comparison_to_twr.twr_ignores_timing: Time-weighted return neutralises cash flow timing — compares manager skill
comparison_to_twr.mwr_includes_timing: Money-weighted return measures what the investor actually earned
